records.summary: Authoring ontology is an intentional policy and capability spine, not a literal copy of user prose or a generator-local enum dump.
records.rationale: The user called out early ontology sketches that combined primitive policy, compound recipe shape, and heuristics. Keep this file as a first-principles authoring vocabulary with comments on keys and visible separation between semantic truth, taste, capability, and recipe policy.
guidance: Before adding authoring operations or taste keys, read ONTOLOGY.md and ontology.ts together.
Annotate ontology keys with intent so future corrections can target the model rather than reverse-engineering names.
Authoring profiles are taste-preference bundles over the same axis/value descriptors used by recipe preferences and orientation rows; keep profile policy out of separate prose-only vocabulary.

Keep support-state rank strict: `observable` means readable app facts, while `plannable` means semantic plans can be constructed. Capability minimum checks should not flatten those promises even if operation support may become plannable once required capabilities are observable.

Capability descriptors may carry product-level open reasons for gaps that are true before opening an app. Keep those global reasons in the ontology/catalog and app-specific reasons in AuthoringOrientation.
Taste axes have a dominant layer, while taste value descriptors carry the exact layer and summary; check value-level layer/confidence/evidence before using a value as policy.

AuthoringOrientation axis rows split primitive-policy, observed-shape, and derived-reading value counts; axis `policyState` is inferred only from primitive-policy values, not from observed-shape facts alone.
AuthoringOrientation taste rows separate ontologySummary from observedSummary; do not collapse durable taste meaning into the current app reading.

AuthoringOrientation `displayText` is a compact transport aid, not a second ontology. It should highlight project shape, key taste values, recipe fit, repair pressure, and open reasons while structured taste/recipe/repair rows remain the precise source of truth. Recipe-fit text should prioritize satisfied recipe identities and summarize partial cross-family candidates instead of presenting them as coequal app identity.

Resource admission taste should use the framework-registration substrate: a direct `.register(...)` call can still admit a runtime-html bundle or plugin configuration, so do not classify it only from call syntax. App-wide resource-registration facts are observed admission shape, not weaker derived style.
Keep modern convention resource declaration out of active ontology until the semantic-runtime actually specifies it.

Keep topology axes narrow: `package-topology` is package/workspace shape, while `source-layout` is roleful source organization. `single-app-package` is observed from source/project shape; feature/bounded-context layout stays a weaker source-organization reading.

Keep template/form/style axes narrow: `template-source-ownership`, `template-rendering-boundary`, `form-value-channel`, `validation-ownership`, `form-type-surface`, `style-resource-ownership`, and `style-binding-model` each answer one question. Recipes can combine them, but the ontology should not.

Keep `style-asset-authoring` separate from broad `design-system` policy. Generated recipes may create and verify component stylesheets through `style-resource` effects without claiming theme tokens, layout policy, accessibility defaults, or component-library integration are solved.

`template-model-access` is the policy vocabulary for clean model access from templates: prefer direct DI state/domain bindings and template-local lookup/narrowing when they remove boilerplate, reserve view-model getters/setters for real adaptation, and treat one-hop forwarding accessors as pressure rather than a pattern to generate.

Orientation should report `direct-state-domain-template-binding` only from AppTopology service-interaction binding rows whose binding root is proven to be an injected state/domain member, `source-backed-getter-observation` only when binding observed-dependency rows show a template read of an ordinary accessor descriptor with a source-backed ComputedObserver path, and `one-hop-forwarding-accessor-pressure` only from template-read, source-proven component accessors whose single return expression is a property chain rooted at an injected state/domain member. Template-local domain reads should still be proven by binding observed-dependency and data-flow expected effects, not by fuzzy path-name heuristics.

`component-interface` should read type surfaces when classifying object-shaped component inputs. Nullable object bindables such as `Product | null` preserve the declared union display but should classify by the effective non-nullable `Product` shape, so object handoff remains visible as a modeling option beside scalar IDs.
`form-type-surface` should distinguish strict form-like value channels from weak/open TypeChecker surfaces so well-typed forms do not look unobserved.

`style-binding-model` values should stay aligned with observer value-channel semantics: whole class-token bindings, per-class toggles, whole style-rule bindings, and per-property style bindings are distinct observed shapes.

anchors.summary: Authoring ontology aggregate.
Human explanation for ontology axes and operation vocabulary.
Pressure fixture for local typed object component inputs and direct domain-object template reads.

records.summary: ExpectedSemanticEffect is the current authoring verification pressure point for describing what authored apps should prove after reopening.
records.rationale: Authoring should not stop at file generation. Recipe and repair plans need expected semantic effects that can disappear, pass, or fail against reopened app facts without turning into brittle snapshots.
guidance: This frontier is nextActionPolicy=when-touched after the expected-effect algebra moved out of `plan.ts`; consult it when adding recipes, repair verification, or new row-backed effect kinds rather than treating the still-large factory class as a standalone cleanup target.
If a recipe cannot name expected semantic effects, its operation is probably still too textual.
Keep semantic effects tied to app facts, topology, bindings, routes, DI, resources, and open seams rather than source snapshots.

Recipe expected-effect coverage is visible through AuthoringCatalog rows as static contracts and through AuthoringOrientation rows as current app observations; rows carry kind sets, counts, `semanticTargetKey`, filter fields and values, while orientation rows add the opened-app observation outcome and observed count from the shared verification primitive. `semanticTargetKey` is for reporting/grouping; recipe-local deduplication uses `expectedSemanticEffectContractKey(...)` so stricter cardinality, filter, capability, or taste variants do not disappear.

Route expected effects preserve the broad topology count when unfiltered, and support row filters such as `originKind` and `valueKind` when recipes need to prove a specific route authoring lane. Use this for routed authoring fixtures before adding route-specific verification code.

`open-seam` is the row-backed expected effect for intentional runtime-boundary pressure fixtures; keep `open-seam-closure` as the absence check for generated/recommendable recipes. Use this split for cases like dynamic router `href`, where one fixture should prove a static route-pattern link closes while a bare external-module href remains an explicit router open seam.

Use the explicit `ExpectedSemanticEffect.atLeast(...)`, `exactly(...)`, and role-specific cardinality constructors for count-sensitive contracts; reserve broad `fact(...)` calls for plain present/absent-like facts or genuinely uncommon combinations.

Use `expectedSemanticEffectsForPlan(plan)` for concrete fixture and repair verification so step-local signature/discriminator effects are checked with the same semantic-target compression as the catalog.

Recipe source-plan coverage is visible through AuthoringCatalog rows without source text: policy fields, complete-text state, file roles, languages, edit kinds, text authorities, file operation ownership, and project-tooling rows. Use precise file roles such as `domain-model` for ordinary entity/value-object files instead of falling through to `other`, so caller-domain adaptation can distinguish model source from state and service boundaries. All current recipe builders expose source and package/typecheck tooling contracts; build-tool profile selection remains host-owned product pressure.

AuthoringCatalog taste-axis rows group common values by `primitive-policy`, `observed-shape`, and `derived-reading` so a caller can see whether an axis has policy-bearing values without reopening ontology.ts.

For authoring-taste expected effects, rows carry the target value layer and ontology summary so recipe-fit pressure can separate primitive policy targets from observed-shape signatures without reopening ontology.ts.

Expected effects carry a role: baseline effects are general app-health checks, signature effects identify candidate recipe shape, and discriminator effects are required recipe-identifying facts. Recipe fit checks unsatisfied discriminators/signatures before unsupported row-backed effects, so a recipe with no satisfied signature-like effects, or an unsatisfied discriminator, is not-applicable rather than a failed or unsupported verification.

ExpectedSemanticEffectFilter predicates match scalar row fields by equality and array row fields by inclusion. Use that for compact fact checks such as `BindingBehaviorApplications.staticArgumentValues`, direct `target-operation` renderer writes, or binding `targetProperty=value` without adding source-text assertions.

Keep primitive form/style expected-effect concepts in `form-expected-effects.ts`; generated recipes should reuse `form-recipe-expected-effects.ts` for standard form-app verification composition, then add only their state, service, validation, or router discriminator effects locally.

Use `readAuthoringVerificationSnapshot(app)` for closed-loop verification from an opened app; it paginates and carries summary, topology, orientation, open seams, runtime-controller rows, runtime compositions, binding-behavior applications, target accesses, target operations, value channels, and data-flow rows so filtered expected effects do not silently fall back to count-only snapshots. Unsupported row-backed projections should fail snapshot construction rather than masquerade as zero observed facts.

`project-tooling` expected effects are backed by project source-role rows. Use them to verify package manifests, TypeScript config files, and module declarations after source plans are applied without comparing file text.

`state-composition` expected effects are backed by `api.AppTopology.stateCompositions`. Use them when a recipe needs to prove that a DI state class owns typed child state instances instead of inferring composition from folder names, object literals, or private implementation fields.

`runtime-composition` expected effects are backed by `api.RuntimeCompositions`. Use them when a recipe needs to prove that an `AuCompose` host resolved dynamic component candidates, compiled their templates, exposed candidate resource-analysis coverage, materialized aggregate composed child-controller handoff for closed branches, closed activation-model handoffs, carried template-only context inputs such as scope behavior, host tag, flush mode, composition, and composing, or fulfilled component/template/model inputs directly versus through promises rather than accepting markup-level composition as closure.

`style-resource` is the fact-level expected effect for stylesheet/style asset ownership rows; keep it separate from `style-resource-ownership`, which is the orientation taste reading derived from those rows.

`template-diagnostic` expected effects are backed by `api.TemplateDiagnostics`. Use them for route-scoped diagnostic/repair witnesses such as weak owner typing and scope-slot typing without snapshotting cursor-info or the whole diagnostics DTO.

`computed-observer-source` and `computed-observer-observed-dependency` expected effects are backed by getter source-observer projection rows. Use them when fixtures or repairs need to prove that ordinary accessor getters would enter ComputedObserver and decorator-owned explicit dependencies would enter ControlledComputedObserver if observed, without confusing those rows with ComputedObservationDefinition metadata or concrete template usage.
Treat expected-effect rows as the verifier-facing contract between fixture generation and reopened app facts.
Use missing effect coverage as a signal to improve semantic-runtime/API projection, not as a reason to generate less ambitious fixtures.

anchors.summary: Expected semantic effect model used by authoring plans.
Verification result model.
API-facing verification snapshot collector for row-backed expected effects.
Shared expected-effect observation primitive used by verification and AuthoringOrientation current-fit rows.
Plan-local expected-effect compression used by recipe catalog rows and fixture verification.
Recipe-level standard form app verification composition.
Shared row-backed runtime-controller expected-effect helper for built-in template-controller hydration.
Getter source-observer product backing computed-observer expected effects.
Route/open-seam contract proving internal string-pattern href closure and deliberate external-module href openness.

records.summary: Use the authoring API, generated fixtures, hand-authored pressure fixtures, external app analysis, official Aurelia docs, and framework tests as one fixture feedback flywheel.
records.rationale: The authoring API should generate recommendable fixtures while the analyzer also learns from stress fixtures and external app patterns that should not become authoring recommendations. Official docs provide promoted-pattern pressure, framework tests provide behavior-grounding examples, and expected semantic effects make generated fixtures meaningful instead of passive snapshots. All lanes should pressure semantic-runtime and Atlas while keeping external app source details out of tracked files.
guidance: Keep generated/recommendable fixtures and stress pressure fixtures in separate folders.

Source-pattern adaptation groups are part of the public recipe/fixture boundary. Forms, wizard flows, dashboards, catalog/table scenarios, and state-store examples should expose grouped parameters when their sample entity, selection, option/data, presentation, composition, validation, or plugin-store pieces must move together.

AuthoringSourcePattern is the source-plan boundary between reusable recipe architecture and complete reference instantiations. Treat domain-neutral shell plans differently from reference-instantiation source text; adapt sample names, fields, data, copy, and presentation before MCP-facing code is emitted for a caller-specific domain. `sourcePlan.pattern.usePolicy` is the compact public action over that boundary: `apply-as-source-start` can scaffold directly, `adapt-before-emitting` is transfer material, `merge-selectively` is companion pattern material, and `analysis-pressure-only` should not become public app source. Source-pattern modules name the reusable architecture capabilities demonstrated by a source plan, while source-pattern parameter rows name adaptation slots for entities, field schemas, collections, selection IDs, route parameters, sample data, and presentation so clients do not infer recipe ontology from sample nouns. Parameter rows also carry `valueShape` so callers can distinguish domain titles, source member names, route paths, route parameter names, route titles, field-schema lists, option-schema lists, collection/action summaries, sample-data summaries, and presentation summaries without learning syntax from fixture text. Prefer precise modules such as route-parameter-selection, route-link-navigation, service-backed-loading/submission, native text/checked/select/matcher channels, capture-attribute-field-shell, and collection search/sort/page/selection controls when a recipe needs the MCP to preserve a particular Aurelia mechanism. Parameter application policy is part of that honesty boundary: `source-text-input` values may be sent through `sourceParameterValues` and applied to generated source, while `advisory-only` values remain host/AI adaptation work until the pattern-module source generator owns them. Searchable table/routed searchable table recipes now apply core entity/collection parameters and supported `table-filter-fields` to source model identity, row fields, filter state, service records, table cells, routed detail rows, expected effects, and table value-channel modules; source-applied table field schemas can remain direct starters with select/boolean facet filters instead of forcing sort, pagination, selection, checked bindings, CSS, or reference presentation. Product/item-like catalog/routed catalog recipes now apply core entity/collection parameters and supported `catalog-fields` to source model identity, item constructor fields, sample records, and routed detail field rows while preserving the recipe-owned card/list state contract through normalized domain getters. Standard request-form recipes now apply core `request-entity`, `request-selection-id`, and supported `request-fields` identity/schema to request/domain class names, scalar ID/bindable names, state/service read/load/submit methods, service class/property names, template-local object handoff, field properties, control bindings, validation targets, expected effects, and source-pattern modules; source-parameterized plain, service-backed, and routed request forms are caller-applied starter plans with structural-baseline styles and no presentation adaptation slot. Starter request-form sample-data rows should be labeled as starter data, draft starters should describe one starter draft rather than two reference records, and public starter templates should use neutral count labels instead of fixture-style plural-marker copy. Unparameterized request forms keep the rich reference scenario. Request-field generation currently covers text, email, secret, textarea, date, number, boolean checkbox/toggle, checked-collection checkbox groups, and single-select controls; date inputs use native `type="date"` value binding, number inputs use Aurelia `value-as-number.bind` instead of string-valued `value.bind`, and checkbox groups use `model.bind` plus `checked.bind` against array membership. `request-options` can now source-apply option-schema lists such as `roles: owner, maintainer; permissions: read, write` when a generated request field schema has select or checked-collection option domains, rewriting option union types, state-owned option arrays, and sample values; omit the `request-options` parameter entirely when the generated field schema has no option-bearing controls. Table-field generation currently covers text, email, number, date, select, and boolean field hints; catalog-field generation currently covers text, number, select, and boolean field hints by mapping them into the stable catalog card contract. Type-compatible natural catalog fields such as `name`, `summary`, `price`, and `inStock` can own the contract member directly; incompatible reserved names and derived slots such as `badge` and `availability` stay behind generated getters or `*Value` properties. Copy, action-model, validation-message copy, sample data, and presentation remain host-adapted work until broader source generators own them; table starters should not expose a presentation adaptation slot when style policy is `none`, and request-form starters should not expose one when style policy is `structural-baseline`. Compact guidance and recipe-plan text should name host-adapted slots explicitly. `applied-to-source-plan` should mean the requested value is reflected in the built parameter row and generated source/tooling text; if not, record recipe-mapping pressure rather than trusting the request. Reference-instantiation source files should also carry `semantic-runtime-reference-instantiation` text authority.

Caller-applied compact starters are not reference-instantiation source. `caller-applied` means the public plan already reflects source parameters; `starter-sample-data` means small generated seed records exist only to make the direct app start runnable. The compact searchable table/list, compact routed searchable table/list, compact catalog, compact routed catalog/list-detail, source-parameterized plain/service/routed request-form profiles, and domain-neutral sectioned route shell are the current canaries: they should report `recommendable-recipe`, `production-terse`, `apply-as-source-start`, semantic-runtime recipe text authority, no reference-instantiation text authority, no unrequested selection state, optional source-owned select/boolean facet filters when caller field parameters request them, no checked taste keys unless explicit selection controls ask for them, no presentation adaptation slot when style policy is `none` or `structural-baseline`, no explanatory placeholder copy inside starter route templates, and direct `state.*`/domain template reads. Compact routed table/catalog starts still need data-driven `load.bind` row links because route-owned detail selection is part of the starter contract; routed request-form starters should keep route identity/viewport/route-context semantics without restoring reference-transfer source authority. Rich table/catalog/routed-table/routed-catalog and unparameterized form plans remain reference instantiations for pressure coverage; do not let their presentation channels leak back into compact MCP-facing starts.

Feature-goal choreography should stay signal-table driven rather than fuzzy search. Keep read/load service integration separate from write/submit integration: `service-boundary` is for service/API/loading/repository integration such as API-backed app data, while `service-write-boundary` is for API-backed save, API-backed status save, backend submit/save, or persist-through-API language. An API-key field with a save button remains plain form-entry guidance unless integration is explicit. Bare native-control words are not enough to activate form-entry because list/table/browser features often use selects, checkboxes, and toggles as filters; require explicit form/settings/editor/profile, preferences, onboarding/wizard, address/payment, API-key, or field-as-field wording. Domain extraction should prefer the requested surface (`board`, `catalog`, `detail`, `compare`, `browser`, `tree`, `viewer`) before field-schema phrases, so values such as `assignee filters`, `category filters`, branch selects, file trees, and request/control fields become advisory field-schema suggestions or the correct listed entity instead of accidental entity names.

Sectioned settings/tabbed-area prompts should surface `sectioned-navigation` as a navigation-frame signal and borrow `routed-app-shell` as the companion pattern. The shell owns static route/viewport/navigation/active-link structure; the form, table, catalog, or wizard recipe still owns the actual domain surface. Do not let a tabbed settings area collapse into one form recipe, and do not pretend the existing form recipe generates a full sectioned app shell. Suppress `form-entry` when a sectioned settings prompt only names section labels; keep `form-entry` when explicit controls such as fields, toggles, selects, save, edit, or validation are present. Settings/preferences draft forms should not invent a scalar ID unless the prompt also names an account/profile/detail/existing editing boundary.

`routed-app-shell` owns a source-applied `section-routes` parameter for static tab/section shells. Use it for prompts such as `routes for dashboard and settings` or `tabs for account, notifications, billing address, and API keys`; it generates one static routeable component/template/navigation link per section and deliberately avoids `IRouteContext`/`itemId` route-parameter source. Keep the default parameterized detail shell for route-param/query/fragment pressure when `section-routes` is omitted.

Feature-goal guidance should keep copyable `sourceParameterValues` as plain `key=value` and publish structured `suggestedSourceParameterContracts` rows, mirrored by a compact contract display line with each suggestion's `valueShape/applicationPolicy`, so MCP clients can tell source-applied route/entity/member identity from advisory field-schema or presentation hints before requesting source text. Keep public guidance code layered: `authoring-guidance.ts` is the answer assembly boundary, `authoring-guidance-selection.ts` owns focus/detail normalization, compact limits, policy-row ranking, and follow-up surfaces, and `authoring-guidance-recipe-row.ts` owns compact recipe/source-pattern/source-parameter contract projection. Table/catalog field-schema suggestions should be normalized descriptors such as `name, assignee select` or `name, description, category select`, not raw phrases such as `assignee filters` or `category filters`; table field suggestions require row-field/filter/sort/column context so global controls like repository branch selectors and surface-only phrases such as `searchable table` do not become row-field schema. `searchable <collection>` phrases name the collection surface, not a `<collection> select` row field. Field/control nouns immediately after a collection surface, such as `table, status filters` or `table, category select`, are field-schema evidence rather than postfix domain heads; keep the domain extractor anchored on clearer entity surfaces such as `editable product details` or plural collection phrases such as `searchable users`. When a mixed plugin/list prompt gives the collection in a `for ...` clause and later names field filters, such as `for project tasks with status filters`, the for-clause domain should own the table entity while the filter phrase stays field-schema evidence. Numeric inventory/quantity/duration phrases such as `stock number`, `seat count number`, `weekly hours number`, or `sla hours number` should preserve their multiword domain labels and survive into both table field descriptors and explicit editable-detail companion form descriptors when a list/detail goal already established table field context. Date-like labels should not duplicate their type marker (`start date`, not `start date date`; `due date`, not `due date date`), date filter phrases should become a clean field descriptor (`due date`) only through the collection/editable-detail bridge rather than a form field named `due date filters`, and multiword amount labels such as `total amount number` should preserve the full domain label in generated source. Editor goals with explicit controls such as role selects, toggles, date fields, or numeric fields should activate form-entry guidance without treating every occurrence of the word editor as a form. Editable-detail companion forms may reuse the table descriptors because the user usually states the domain fields once, but keep that bridge gated to explicit editable-detail intent so list filters do not become form fields. Product/item/tier catalog wording and exact product-tier/pricing-tier prompts may use catalog recipes, but product/admin table plus editable-detail wording should stay on the searchable-table/form path unless storefront/catalog/card/cart/checkout/pricing/compare intent is also present, and non-commerce catalog domains remain conservative until the recipe is generic.

Routed form source plans should derive default route sample parameter values from the adapted request domain (`task-1`, `team-member-1`) rather than carrying the reference `request-1`; route links, redirect targets, service sample records, and summary copy should move together when `request-entity` or `request-route-parameter` are source-applied.

`multi-step-state-backed-form` now source-applies `wizard-entity` to the composed domain class, state property, validation rule target, direct template binding paths, root title, completion count, computed-observer expected effects, and binding data-flow expected effects. Keep `wizard-steps` advisory-only until a richer step/section schema can generate arbitrary workflow sections honestly.

Recipe source plans should expose role-driven `textRequestHints`, and authoring recipe-plan requests should accept matching `sourceTextRequestHintKeys`, so MCP clients can request `implementation-source` without reference CSS, state/domain/service, template, entry/component shell, presentation, or project-tooling paths without copying parameterized generated file names. Treat presentation hints as opt-in reference CSS/copy rather than reusable recipe policy.

Keep reference presentation and public source-pattern declarations physically separate from concrete source-plan file generation when a recipe grows beyond shell scale. The searchable table and routed searchable table recipes keep their reference CSS in `searchable-data-table-reference-presentation.ts` and parameter/module/adaptation-group semantics in `searchable-data-table-source-pattern.ts`; catalog and routed catalog recipes keep their reference CSS in `catalog-storefront-reference-presentation.ts` and source-pattern semantics in `catalog-storefront-source-pattern.ts`. Shared list/detail route identity parameter rows and adaptation groups live in `route-source-pattern.ts` so routed recipes do not fork route-slot semantics while their domain/schema groups remain recipe-local. When routed catalog source parameters supply a caller collection/entity/field schema and explicit route identity values are omitted, route path/title/detail parameter/sample navigation defaults should derive coherently from the applied domain model instead of falling back to the reference `products` route. The source-plan modules should own concrete source file assembly.

Source-parameter application verification should be value-shape aware. Domain-title values may be reflected as title, lower-title, Pascal, camel, kebab, or snake source forms; source/member/route shapes should preserve their own identifier or text expectations. Do not regress to raw substring-only checks when deciding `applied-to-source-plan`.
Use AuthoringOrientation before fixture expansion so recipes, capability gaps, taste/policy choices, and expected semantic effects are visible.

Use `SemanticRuntime.authoringCatalog()` or the equivalent AuthoringCatalog app query when the question is static vocabulary or recipe contracts; use AuthoringOrientation when the question is how a currently opened app satisfies those contracts.

For broad fixture sweeps, run semantic-runtime `pressure:app-api`; it discovers generated authoring fixtures and pressure fixture folders, then defaults to aggregate compact output so expected-effect target outcome buckets explain recipe-fit failures without per-root transcript noise. Compact output starts with `inputs.fixture-lanes` and `inputs.fixture-intent-recipes` so generated authoring fixtures, hand-authored authoring fixtures, and stress pressure fixtures are not conflated. Use `SEMANTIC_RUNTIME_PRESSURE_DETAIL=summary` or `raw` only after the compact pressure buckets identify a thread that needs wider detail. The intent-recipe outcome lane is the generated-fixture health signal, the applicable-recipe lane keeps partial recipe candidates visible, and the all-recipe lane intentionally keeps cross-recipe failed effects as contrast. Use `SEMANTIC_RUNTIME_PRESSURE_OUTPUT=inputs` only for isolated-root inspection.

In compact `pressure:app-api` output, treat `authoring.intent-*` rows as active generated fixture pressure. Hand-authored authoring fixtures can deliberately have no intent recipe even when they are recommendable app pressure. Treat `authoring.applicable-*` rows as recipe-selection or repair pressure because partial candidates may share app-shell signatures without being the fixture's intended recipe. Treat `authoring.all-candidate-*` rows as contrastive recipe-selection context. Do not widen recipes or fixtures solely because a non-intent row includes failed effects from another recipe family.

When `SEMANTIC_RUNTIME_PRESSURE_ROOTS` is set to the authoring fixture root or pressure fixture root, app pressure expands the collection root into child fixture inputs and de-duplicates explicit child roots. Use explicit child roots only when one fixture should be inspected as a single input.

When `project-tooling` expected effects look failed in a broad sweep, read `authoring.intent-project-tooling-fixture-lane-outcomes` before changing recipes or fixture materialization. Generated fixtures should satisfy those rows; stress fixtures may legitimately lack local package/typecheck files and should be interpreted as analysis contrast pressure.

When broad app pressure shows source-assignment, template missing-input, framework error-code, or open-seam rows, read the fixture-owner cross-tabs (`bindings.data-flow-assignment-reason-fixtures`, `templates.missing-input-fixtures`, `templates.diagnostic-error-code-fixtures`, `app.diagnostic-error-code-fixtures`, `bindings.data-flow-error-code-fixtures`, `router.error-code-fixtures`, `open-seams.reason-fixtures`) before doing per-fixture runs. Internal fixtures use public `authoring:<folder>` and `pressure:<folder>` keys; custom/external roots collapse to `custom-root`.

Read the axis-keyed authoring taste pressure rows before opening raw orientation payloads; they show which narrow axes are unobserved, which values appeared under which axis, and where a recipe-fit failure is only a missing signature value. `pressure:app-api` reports `policyState` under primitive-policy headings because observed-shape and derived-reading values can verify recipe signatures without becoming edit policy. It also prints focused taste sections for style binding, style ownership, form value channels, state ownership, validation ownership, and build-tool profile so low-count but important values do not disappear under the compact top-N rollup.

Generated state/validated-state/service/routed form recipes intentionally include a root component stylesheet via `create-style-asset`, a `canSubmit` class-token binding, a capture-based `field-shell` custom element that forwards native `value` and validation attributes through `...$attrs`, checked/radio bindings, a nullable single-select with `option model.bind="null"`, and multi-select bindings, with `style-resource`, `component-stylesheet`, `class-token-binding`, filtered binding target-access/value-channel/data-flow verification, primitive `null` value-domain verification, a direct `target-operation` expectation for captured `type="email"` spreading into the inner native input, and a captured `valueSiteKind=captured-value` two-way data-flow expectation for forwarded value bindings. State-backed variants also carry one object-valued assignee select with `matcher.bind="state.sameSupportAgent"`; expected effects verify `request.assignee` direct data-flow/observed-dependency rows and a `usesCustomMatcher` value-channel row without turning object comparison into a universal form policy. Native value filters use `targetKind=node` plus `targetProperty=value`; captured value flow filters additionally use `direction=two-way`, `valueChannelKind=raw-property`, and `sourceAssignmentKind=runtime-assignable`; checked filters use `targetKind=node` plus `targetProperty=checked`; custom element `value` bindables do not satisfy native-control expectations unless capture/spread rendering materializes the inner native target.

State-backed request expected effects now flow through a shared field-effect descriptor lane for both default recipe fields and caller-supplied `request-fields`. Keep direct request-member data-flow, direct observed-dependency, and `canSubmit` getter dependency expectations on that shared path when adding form controls, so default fields and source-parameterized fields do not drift.

Plain/localized/validated/service/routed state-backed form components keep a scalar `requestId` interface and use template-local `<let request.bind="state.readRequest(requestId)"></let>` plus `if.bind` narrowing for the id-to-domain-object adaptation boundary. Do not reintroduce component-level `request` getters or per-field forwarding accessors just to shorten template expressions. Their expected effects verify exact `LetBinding` scope-slot data-flow from `state.readRequest(requestId)` into the template-local `request`, exact direct request binding rows for `request.customerName`/native value, `request.email`/native value, `request.urgent`/`checked-boolean`, `request.contactPreference`/`checked-radio-value`, `request.primaryTopic`/`select-single-option-value`, `request.assignee`/`select-single-option-value` with `usesCustomMatcher=true`, `request.topics`/`select-multiple-option-values`, plus matching `binding-observed-dependency` member reads. Treat failures there as LetBinding scope, topology/style-recognition, recursive rendering/scope, or binding value-channel/data-flow pressure, not as expendable decoration.

The service-backed form recipe uses the same `<let>` request adaptation boundary so controls bind directly to object members such as `customerName`, `email`, `urgent`, and select fields. That direct object-backed scope is intentional recommendable pressure for ordinary domain-object binding and runtime LetBinding scope handoff; expected effects should prove the object-backed field flows and state/service interactions instead of restoring per-field component setters.

Checked/select custom matcher presence is now an authoring-observable `form-value-channel:custom-matcher-comparison` taste and can be verified with a `binding-value-channel` expected effect filtered by `usesCustomMatcher=true`. Do not execute matcher bodies in authoring orientation; behavior analysis remains a separate TypeChecker/evaluator pressure.

`localized-state-backed-form` extends the standard generated form lane with `@aurelia/i18n` plugin registration, static `I18nConfiguration` resources, `t` and `t-params.bind` template usage, `i18n-translation-key` expected effects over `api.I18nTranslationKeys`, and `i18n-translation-binding` expected effects over rendered `api.I18nTranslationBindings` target groups. Treat failures there as i18n catalog/query/effect pressure or translation-binding lifecycle pressure; dynamic backend loading and runtime language switching remain separate frontiers.

`localized-validated-state-backed-form` is the common combined plugin form recipe: it layers static i18n resources/rendered translation bindings and validation-html rules/`& validate:'blur'` rows over the same DI-owned request model. Use it for MCP-facing translated validated form guidance, while keeping localized-only and validated-only recipes available when a feature only needs one plugin lane.

`routed-localized-validated-state-backed-form` is the route-owned combined plugin form recipe: it keeps route-param selected state and static router navigation/query/fragment verification together with i18n translation rows and validation-html rows over the same DI-owned request model. Use it for MCP-facing edit/create form guidance that needs routing, translation, and validation without introducing view-model forwarding facades.

`multi-step-state-backed-form` is the generated wizard/progress form recipe: it keeps progression, readiness, and profile data in DI-owned state/domain classes, uses repeat/if template-controller rows for step rendering, binds directly to `state.profile.*` without forwarding accessors, verifies validation-html usage, native value/checked/select channels, checked collection membership, class-token/class-toggle channels, style-rule interpolation, state-composition, service-interaction bindings, and plain getter ComputedObserver rows. Generated recipes that resolve the same state class from multiple components rely on Aurelia's default root singleton resolver for ordinary constructable classes, not per-component duplicated state. Use Atlas corpus `classificationKey=multi-step-form` to ground future wizard-like pressure before widening the recipe.

AuCompose component values that are statically visible plain objects, instances, boundary objects, or non-resource constructables should resolve as `object-view-model`, not as an open custom-element lookup failure. Object-view-model rows reuse the TypeChecker-backed `activate(model)` assignability path, including constructable inputs whose activate method lives on the instance type. Custom-element candidate rows additionally own compiled-template and candidate-runtime-analysis coverage.

Shared recommendable app plan steps live in `recipe-plan-steps.ts`; use those helpers for stable project files, entrypoint, root component, stylesheet, external template, plugin, plain domain model, state model, service, route, component, template binding, and verification operation-to-step boundaries, then keep recipe-specific expected effects and route/form discriminator choices local to the concrete recipe.

`service-backed-form` is now service-backed state rather than a direct component-facing service facade: generated components resolve DI state, state resolves a service/repository boundary for loading and submission side effects, expected effects prove component-to-state binding handoff plus state-to-service calls, and direct component-to-service facades should be treated as observed app shape or contrastive pressure rather than the recommendable default.

`catalog-storefront` is the first generated app-building recipe beyond forms: generated components use local typed product-object handoff for the list/card boundary, DI-owned composed state, a state-owned service boundary, direct `state.*` and `product.*` bindings, native search/select/checked filter controls bound directly to nested catalog state, plain getter projections over entity lookups and collection-derived state, service interaction bindings, `state-composition` effects, row-backed conditional/repeat runtime-controller effects, promise owner plus linked `pending`/`then`/`catch` runtime-controller effects, switch owner plus linked `case`/`default-case` runtime-controller effects, class-token interpolation, style-rule interpolation, per-class `.class` toggles, per-property `.style` bindings, and zero-open-seam verification. Its expected effects prove direct DI-state projection joins, product-object component binding data flow, product-domain binding data flow, filter value/checked/select target access, value channels, data flow, debounce behavior, direct filter observed dependencies, plain getter `ComputedObserver` source rows plus observed proxy collection dependency rows, class/style target access, value-channel, data-flow, and narrow style-binding taste rows instead of only an orientation taste. Route/lazy selection can still use scalar identity when navigation or loading owns the boundary.

`routed-app-shell` is the generic route-shell recipe: it registers RouterConfiguration, declares decorator route config, emits static load links plus a named au-viewport, reads route params/query values through IRouteContext, and verifies router options, route config, route recognizer products, query/fragment propagation, route-node aggregation, RouteContext/viewport/component-agent rows, routeable component roles, and zero open seams without importing a form, catalog, or data-table domain model. Use it as the MCP pattern-reference companion when a routed feature starts from another non-routed recipe.

`routed-catalog-storefront` is the first larger routed app-building recipe: it composes catalog-storefront state/service/component/filter patterns with list/detail route config, `products/:productId`, root static detail navigation with query and fragment values, data-driven product-card links, `IRouteContext` parameter reads, router `activeClass` for class-only active link styling, route-node/viewport/component-agent effects, local product-object card handoff, route-parameter selected detail state, direct product-domain detail/card bindings, and the catalog binding/observation/service-interaction contract. Source-parameterized compact routed-catalog starts should stay `apply-as-source-start` and presentation-free while preserving route-detail read-by-id and row detail links; unparameterized/rich plans remain reference instantiations for card object handoff, selection, stock/badge filters, promise/switch controllers, and class/style pressure. Reusable routed child components should use root-relative or parent-relative route instructions deliberately when linking outside their local route context. Use it as the first MCP-facing recipe canary for larger app scaffolding, while treating broader route-link closure, `load.active.bind` state handoff, guards, lifecycle hooks, and deeper viewport parity as router substrate pressure unless the app genuinely needs those semantics.

`searchable-data-table` is the generated low-boilerplate data-grid recipe: it uses a table component that resolves DI-owned composed state, state-owned service-backed loading, direct `state.*` and `user.*` template reads, native `value.bind`/select option `model.bind`/checkbox `checked.bind` channels, `& debounce`, keyed repeats, class-token/class-toggle/style channels, and ordinary getter ComputedObserver rows for filter/sort/page projections. Selection deliberately uses `Set<number>` because the framework CheckedObserver supports Array, Set, and Map mutation and the template compiler reorders checked/model/value/matcher instructions so the checked observer sees element model identity. Use it as the MCP-facing search/filter/sort/pagination/selection canary when authoring larger list-management surfaces; do not replace those value channels with manual DOM event plumbing or add view-model forwarding accessors merely to shorten state paths. Built-in template-controller value channels from `if`/`repeat` are template-composition evidence, not custom-control-binding taste.

`routed-searchable-data-table` composes the data-grid recipe with common list/detail routing: the root registers RouterConfiguration, the list route hosts the table, row links use data-driven `load.bind` route params, the detail route owns IRouteContext parameter/query adaptation, and both routes share the same DI-owned table state/service loading boundary. Source-parameterized compact routed-table starts should stay `apply-as-source-start` and presentation-free while preserving row detail links; unparameterized/rich plans remain reference instantiations for search/filter/sort/pagination/selection and class/style pressure. Use it as the MCP-facing management-feature canary when search/filter/sort/pagination and route-owned detail selection must be planned together without forwarding getters or duplicated state.

`composed-dashboard` is the first generated dynamic-composition recipe: it uses DI-owned widget state, repeated dashboard cards, a TypeChecker-visible union of widget component classes, `AuCompose` component/model handoff, async flush mode, a scoped template-only composition summary, promise-valued component/template inputs, a static string-component preview for closed-branch composed child-controller handoff, `runtime-composition` expected effects, row-backed repeat runtime-controller effects, direct activated-model widget template reads, source-backed getter-observation effects, and the `dynamic-component-composition` taste. Its runtime-composition effects check `activationHandoffKinds=model-assignable`, static/defaulted scope/flush/tag context fields, direct/promise/absent/open input fulfillment, `composition`/`composing` from-view binding presence, and `composedChildControllerCount=1`, so generated fixtures prove the widget model type reaches `activate(model)`, promise-valued inputs are unwrapped through the evaluator lane, static component handoff creates a modeled child controller, template-only composition inputs are modeled rather than only proving component lookup, and widget components bind through `model.items` / `model.points` instead of generating one-hop forwarding getters.

`state-store-list` is the generated @aurelia/state canary and should stay an explicit plugin-state choice rather than the generic state default: it registers StateDefaultConfiguration.init(...).withStore(...), proves default and named state-store rows, plugin-registration taste, aurelia-state-store ownership taste, state binding-behavior applications, typed & state/.state data flow through StateBindingScopeProjector, state-dispatch-action payload value channels, store-scope observed dependencies, readable binary predicate display such as `draft === ""`, and input $event.target.value payload typing through the authored native input. `store-item` and `store-collection` are source-applicable for caller-named store-list identity; reducer action modeling, sample data, and presentation remain host-adapted until broader store-shape generation exists. Recommendable & state usage may appear in bind-command expressions or interpolation holes: runtime-html binds each interpolation part through InterpolationPartBinding, so the framework binding-behavior bind phase can install the store-backed scope for text interpolation too. Remaining state-command pressure is dynamic store-name selection, broader non-form event-target policy, reducer action-shape generation, and action payload target-type policy.

Durable generated authoring fixtures should materialize the full recipe source plan, including package manifest, TypeScript config, and local asset declarations, so app-pressure sees project-tooling expected effects and the observed `build-tool-profile:typecheck-only-tooling` taste instead of only the `src/` tree. The fixture materializer derives from `AuthoringRecipeDescriptors`, resets each generated fixture folder before writing, and should be run with `pnpm --filter @aurelia-ls/semantic-runtime fixtures:authoring` after recipe source-plan changes.

Generated authoring recipes should import core app APIs from the public `aurelia` package (`Aurelia`, `customElement`, `bindable`, `resolve`) and keep plugin packages explicit only when used. This aligns MCP-facing source output with the public scaffold while semantic-runtime still models the lower-level StandardConfiguration defaults through framework facade admission.

The public `npx makes aurelia` scaffold is useful migration/scaffold pressure, not complete app-design policy. Its current routed app shape uses the public `aurelia` facade, optional `RouterConfiguration`, `.app(MyApp).start()`, a route-decorated app shell, and ordinary anchor navigation examples; use it to keep MCP source familiar while letting semantic-runtime recipes add stronger DI-state, observation, and expected-effect contracts.

`convention-minimal-app` is the code-economy app-shell canary for current Aurelia convention resource discovery: it uses the public `aurelia` entrypoint plus a convention-compatible class/source/template pair instead of a root `customElement` decorator. Use it when the class/file/template naming pair is provable through `resource-convention.ts`; keep explicit decorator recipes for components that need dependencies, bindables, capture, shadow options, or other metadata conventions do not express.

Use `pnpm --filter @aurelia-ls/semantic-runtime check:authoring-fixtures` after source-plan changes that affect generated TypeScript. It is a repo-local generated-code canary that maps `aurelia` and `@aurelia/*` imports to in-repo framework declaration output; it complements semantic smokes and app-pressure expected effects, but does not prove standalone package installation or runtime bundling.

`validated-state-backed-form` is the recommendable validation recipe: it adds ValidationHtmlConfiguration, validation service resolution, source-authored lambda rules over the `ServiceRequest` class, default `& validate:'blur'` bindings to `request.*` values, `validation-errors.from-view` presentation regions, direct error-array-driven field presentation classes, fact-level `binding-behavior-application` verification for `validate`, controller-view-model `errors` binding target/value/data-flow verification for validation-errors, class data-flow verification from validation error arrays into field presentation, and a `validation-controller-usage` discriminator taste while keeping the plain form recipe unopinionated. Aurelia's validation accessor parser accepts property chains, not arbitrary state lookup calls, so do not restore a component `request` getter solely for validation metadata. Hosts can still request plain `& validate` by passing `validationTrigger: null`; verify static triggers through `BindingBehaviorApplications.staticArgumentValues` rather than source text.
When a generated fixture exposes a substrate gap, deepen the substrate before papering over the recipe.

Use `routed-service-backed-form` when the authoring loop needs a recommendable form that combines route-param selected state, background service/repository loading, DI-owned state, template-local request lookup, and direct domain-object bindings.

Generated form fixtures that report scope-slot assignment strictness are a substrate canary: first verify whether binding data-flow writeability is missing a view-model context-type fallback before changing recipe source.

Wrapper-component fixture failures around captured `...$attrs` are recursive runtime/scope canaries: verify child custom-element rendering, child compiled-template scope walking, dynamic instruction origin claims, and conditional branch pass-through scopes before changing fixture markup.
Use official Aurelia docs and framework tests as pressure inputs through Atlas corpus lenses; docs frequency is not idiom policy and tests are not fixture source to copy wholesale.

Atlas framework corpus fixture seeds now include `binding-observed-dependency` when a snippet has concrete template binding or interpolation expression surfaces. Use `effectKind=binding-observed-dependency` with exact surface classification filters to find docs/tests pressure for direct state/domain reads instead of treating observed-dependency expectations as recipe-local-only facts.

Atlas framework corpus fixture seeds split `computed-observation-definition` from getter-side `computed-observer-source` and `computed-observer-observed-dependency`. Method-level `@computed` is trackable-method declaration pressure; only ordinary or decorator-owned getters seed ComputedObserverSource rows, including the plain-getter bridge when a getter calls a computed method.

When selecting form fixture seeds from docs/tests, prefer exact Atlas corpus classification filters such as `classificationKind=surface` plus `classificationKey=native-value-binding`, `native-checked-binding`, `option-model-binding`, or `validation-binding-behavior` before broad prose queries. These surface keys point at local doc fences or concrete test behavior snippets; `describe(...)` suite wrappers remain in test-snippets for overview but are not fixture seeds.

Treat object-valued select plus `matcher.bind` as part of the standard generated form canary across plain, service-backed, routed, and plugin-composed forms. The source plan should prove `usesCustomMatcher` value-channel rows and custom-matcher taste without making object comparison mandatory for every select.
When an external app or stress fixture exposes weak typing or plugin/router/i18n pressure, generalize the pattern into Atlas or semantic-runtime without committing external app paths or templates.

anchors.summary: Recommendable authoring fixture lane.
Authoring-facing orientation read surface.
Expected semantic effects used to compare generated intent with reopened app facts.
Shared standard authoring recipe plan-step constructors.
Current aggregate docs/tests corpus pressure lane.
Generated convention minimal app shell pressure smoke.
Generated state-backed form pressure smoke.
Generated validated state-backed form pressure smoke.
Generated multi-step state-backed form pressure smoke.
Generated localized state-backed form pressure smoke.
Generated combined i18n and validation form pressure smoke.
Generated routed service-backed form, route-selected state, and service-boundary pressure smoke.
Generated catalog storefront app-building pressure smoke.
Generated generic route-shell and router pressure smoke.
Generated routed catalog storefront app-building and router pressure smoke.
Generated searchable data-table app-building and value-channel pressure smoke.
Generated routed searchable data-table app-building and router pressure smoke.
Generated routed i18n and validation form pressure smoke.
Generated composed dashboard dynamic-composition pressure smoke.
Generated @aurelia/state store and binding-behavior pressure smoke.
Public makes/npx scaffold pressure for familiar Aurelia app-shell and router source shape.
Combined app API pressure surface with authoring expected-effect target outcomes.
Generated recipe fixture TypeScript canary using repo-local Aurelia declaration mappings.

records.summary: Authoring source plans are the explicit source-edit boundary until concrete edit application, formatting, and host tooling policy are modeled.
records.rationale: Recipes can attach file-level source plans, but source edit application is still outside semantic-runtime. Keeping this boundary explicit prevents operation prose from silently becoming a generator or code-action engine.
guidance: Use source-plan.ts for recipe-owned source text authority, conflict policy, formatting policy, and package-tooling policy.

Use package-tooling.ts for recipe-baseline package manifests, TypeScript config, declaration files, dependencies, and scripts; build-tool profile and package-manager execution policy remain host-owned until modeled.

Use `configuredAureliaEntrypointFile(...)` for public `Aurelia.app(...).start()` entrypoints with optional plugin/router registrations instead of adding recipe-local entrypoint templates. This keeps MCP-facing source terse and makes repeated registration-chain formatting one source-plan concern.

Keep recipe source plans split around named file-artifact producers once they grow beyond a minimal app shell. Functions such as `stateBackedFormComponentFile(...)` or `serviceBackedFormStateFile(...)` give Atlas and repair planning stable handles for one generated artifact without inventing a generic source-plan builder.
Do not hide edit application policy inside operation names or recipe prose.
When edit application becomes real, first model the host boundary and verification effects rather than bolting writes onto recipes.

anchors.summary: Current source edit plan boundary.
Shared public Aurelia entrypoint source-plan helper for optional registration chains.
Structured project-tooling boundary for recipe-baseline package/typecheck artifacts.
Recipe descriptor surface.

records.summary: AuthoringCatalog and AuthoringOrientation split static authoring contracts from opened-app evidence over capability, taste, recipe, repair, and fixture expected-effect rows.
records.rationale: The north-star correction is that authoring, generated fixtures, semantic-runtime analysis, and Atlas visibility form one bootstrapping loop. The stable read side now has two lanes: a static catalog for ontology and recipe contracts, and orientation for what the opened app can observe, author, verify, or repair before source-edit/generator machinery grows.
guidance: Start here before widening authoring operations, fixture generation, or MCP packaging.
Read `SemanticRuntime.authoringCatalog()` or `SemanticAppQueryKind.AuthoringCatalog` before orientation when future work needs the static ontology/recipe contract without app-specific evidence.

Use the catalog taste-axis value-layer splits before treating an observed-shape taste value as policy. Primitive-policy values may guide generation; observed-shape and derived-reading values are evidence unless a profile or user preference elevates them.
Use catalog profile preference rows for decorator, convention, and explicit-registration policy. If a profile has no preference rows, treat it as an ambiguity summary, not hidden generation policy.

Use catalog recipe preference rows for the seed plan's intended taste choices, then compare them with opened-app orientation taste rows before deciding whether a fixture matched, drifted, or needs repair.

Build-tool selection lives on the `build-tool-profile` taste axis. Current generated recipes prefer `host-selected-build-tool`; project tooling may observe typecheck-only or bundler config shapes, but semantic-runtime should not choose a runnable build profile until that contract is explicit.

Use catalog capability/operation product open reasons to identify global source-edit, package-tooling, or product-area gaps before treating opened-app capability open reasons as fixture-specific pressure.
Keep orientation rows evidence-backed by current app facts, resources, bindings, routes, DI, diagnostics, and open seams.

Keep orientation coverage/surface rows aligned with verifier row-backed effects and app-opening facts: resource visibility, runtime controllers, binding target accesses, direct target operations, binding value channels, binding behavior applications, and binding data flows should remain separate surfaces because they answer different authoring questions.

Recipe rows should expose compact expected-effect rows from concrete builders, including scope, cardinality, filter fields and values, capability targets, taste targets, current observed count, and current outcome, so fixture expansion can compare recipe ambition with verifier/API coverage.
Fixture smokes should verify `expectedSemanticEffectsForPlan(plan)` rather than only the last plan step; otherwise catalog-visible signature and discriminator effects can be skipped accidentally.
Use `readAuthoringVerificationSnapshot(app)` when a fixture, repair, or authoring loop needs closed-loop verification; do not reassemble the row-backed verification basis in individual smoke scripts.

Recipe rows should expose source-plan contract rows when a plan has concrete source artifacts, but not file text; use those rows to separate editable recipe coverage from host-owned source edit/package tooling policy.
Recipe source templates should fail closed on missing or unused placeholders so source-plan drift is caught before generated fixtures are reopened.

Recipe source-plan rows now carry project-tooling subrows for package dependencies, scripts, config file kinds, and build-tool policy. Current recipes provide package/typecheck baselines while leaving build-tool profile selection open.

Package-tooling capability state should be partial or observable, not fully open, once recipe-baseline package/typecheck tooling exists; keep build-tool and package-manager execution open through product reasons instead of blocking all app-shell operations.

Generated recipes should verify both project-tooling source roles and the observed `build-tool-profile:typecheck-only-tooling` taste, while keeping `host-selected-build-tool` as the primitive recipe preference.

Public app-building guidance can accept a natural-language `featureGoal`, but matching must remain an explicit authoring signal table rather than fuzzy search. Mixed goals such as routed searchable translated validation should surface combined recipes and route/component/plugin decisions in the compact first read.

Compact authoring guidance should keep focused recipe rows bounded; open `detail=recipes` or pass an explicit recipe limit when the task is recipe comparison rather than first-screen app-building guidance.

Compact generated catalog/table fixtures are source-parameterized canaries, not static base-recipe intent proofs. Keep `catalog-storefront:compact` and `searchable-data-table:compact` distinct in app-pressure; the pressure lane now rebuilds the concrete generated fixture plans with applied source parameters and verifies their expected effects directly instead of relying on static orientation recipe rows.

Feature-goal term activation should use normalized high-signal token/phrase matching, not substring search, so explicit signal rows do not drift into fuzzy routing behavior or treat generic domain nouns like `messages` and `labels` as capability intent.
Feature-goal display text should make the no-match fallback explicit; `none matched` means the following recipe order is focus/default guidance rather than a specific feature-signal path.

Feature-goal signals should retain planning-layer intent. A mixed table plus translated validation feature should not force one mega-recipe: use `recipePlanSequence` to start from the main architecture-choice or feature-surface recipe and add companion recipe plans for capability layers that still need source-plan guidance.

`recipePlanSequence` companion rows should be `pattern-reference` usage. They point an authoring agent at relevant recipe steps/effects to merge into the primary source plan, not at a second full generated app.
Recipe-plan reads should carry `usage=pattern-reference` when opened from a companion recipe path row so public text, recipe intent, and source-plan merge semantics stay aligned.

Navigation ownership taste is split across route origin and value-shape dimensions. Origin-shaped values include `decorator-route-config`, `configure-call-route-config`, `class-static-default-route-config`, and `child-routes-property-route-config`; `static-route-config` is the closed value-shape reading, not proof of one source syntax.
When reading recipe rows from arbitrary opened apps, prefer applicable-recipe and candidate-signature outcome buckets before interpreting all-recipe baseline missing facts as product pressure.

Use recipe `currentFitState` plus signature and discriminator satisfied/failed/unsupported counts as a first-pass orientation signal. `not-applicable` means no signature-like effect matched or a discriminator failed; it is not a verification failure. Recipe rows also carry base recipes, lineage, and specificity rank so richer recipes can satisfy base shapes without becoming indistinguishable from them. This is not a replacement for source planning or repair policy.
Separate recommendable generated fixtures from stress pressure fixtures, then compare expected semantic effects against reopened app facts.

Repair clusters must split by concrete action-target source when one exists; high-volume weak-owner diagnostics that all display as any still need one source-owner-type-strengthening cluster per actionable owner surface.

For missing scope-slot typing, use the evaluator's open subject as the repair action target. The diagnostic member span and selected member names are evidence; the scope slot is the surface to repair.
If orientation cannot explain a recipe or repair gap, improve semantic-runtime substrate or Atlas visibility before adding generator-local policy.

anchors.summary: Read-only authoring orientation query.
Static authoring ontology and recipe contract projection.
Semantic authoring loop boundary.
Flexible authoring scope map.

records.summary: AuthoringCatalog product open reasons are static product gaps, not app-specific recipe failures.
records.rationale: The catalog intentionally reports package-tooling/source-edit policy gaps before an app is opened, while recipe fit and verification outcomes live in AuthoringOrientation and verification snapshots. A recipe can therefore be editable and still carry `package-tooling-policy-open` until runnable build-tool/package-manager execution policy has a contract.
guidance: Do not treat editable recipes with only `package-tooling-policy-open` as stale or failed; use that reason to remember that recipe-baseline package/typecheck tooling is not a runnable build profile.

`routed-state-backed-form` now has an editable source plan, verified effects for route config, static request-parameter names, decoded request-parameter values, static query params, repeated query-key arrays, fragment propagation, child-first route-node parameter aggregation, sibling named viewport layout, route tree, recognizer, and component-agent topology, and a verifiable router capability row for that modeled subset. The separate `router-route-parameter-aggregation` stress fixture proves recursive residual child-route recognition, nested child-first/parent-first/append/by-route aggregation, closed static query-param inclusion, duplicate query-key arrays, and fragment propagation without making repeated parameter names a generated recommendation. Router authoring remains a broader substrate frontier for guards, lifecycle hooks, dynamic query merging, viewport activation, and repair/autofix policy, but this generated fixture should satisfy its declared route effects.

If catalog open reasons feel surprising, inspect `AuthoringCatalog` first for static product gaps and `AuthoringOrientation` second for opened-app evidence before adding recipe-local workaround policy.

anchors.summary: Static authoring ontology and recipe contract projection.
Recipe support states and product open reasons.

anchors.heading: Algebra
anchors.summary: Authoring ontology explanation for support states, taste policy, and package-tooling policy.

records.summary: Mixed feature-goal guidance needs surface-specific domain extraction before suggesting source parameters.
records.rationale: Public MCP authoring prompts often combine a collection surface, form surface, framework capability, and route shell in one request. A single inferred domain for every companion recipe can make generated source feel literal or wrong, such as treating localization as the app domain or applying an audit-log table domain to a profile-settings form.
guidance: Keep feature-goal source-parameter values deterministic suggestions, not semantic truth; clients should review them before passing them to recipe plans.
Use collection-domain extraction for table/list/catalog recipes and request-form-domain extraction for form companions when a prompt contains multiple surfaces.

Reserve postfix domain extraction for source-shaped phrases such as `searchable projects` or `table products`; if the same surface already has a leading domain phrase, treat the following tokens as the next feature/capability clause.
When adding new feature-surface extractors, verify mixed prompts where framework capabilities such as localization, validation, or API-backed save appear after the collection surface.

anchors.summary: Routes recipe-specific source-parameter suggestions through collection, request-form, multi-step, shell, and catalog domain extraction.
Deterministic surface-token domain extractor used for source-parameter suggestions.
Contains the mixed authenticated-admin prompt canary for collection/form domain separation.
